notes:
- Key concepts introduced
  - Open Router access example: a new log entry "R10528" indicating a call routed via Open Router (demonstrates the call was made and cost was zero).
  - Open Router service: provides access to many models via providers so you don't need to host models locally; free models require no credit card, paid models require payment and will fail/ask for money if used without payment.
  - Free-model discovery: filtering models by "free" in the models list to find zero-cost models.
  - Three model-usage options (the "three models" the speaker highlights):
    - ChatGPT (paid option) used as the primary/remote model.
    - Local LLaMA (local hosting) for users wanting data privacy / everything on their system.
    - Open Router (third-party service) where you obtain a key and choose among multiple models.
  - Multimodal input (corrected from "multimodel input"): enabling agents to accept more than text (e.g., images) so agents can read/learn from images and other non-text inputs.
  - Structured output: expecting model responses in a structured format (JSON) useful for APIs, web apps, and deterministic downstream parsing.
  - Model capability variance: not all models support image/multimodal inputs; older models (e.g., GPT-3.5 Turbo) and some smaller models (Tiny Llama) lack image support; newer models (e.g., GPT-4, DeepS v3 via Open Router) have image support.
  - Autogen message types: agents communicate via messages; message types include text message, image message, tool message (and chat/multimodal variants).
  - Task result: an important concept/type to be used later (referenced as part of autogen/API).
  - Practical point: you can send an image and get textual or JSON responses from multimodal-capable models.
  - Usability/caveat: syntax for using Open Router or message formats can be tricky — the speaker took time to learn it and recommends copying the correct syntax.

- Analogies or metaphors used
  - Agents communicating compared to how we text our friends.
  - Ten years ago: simple SMS; now messaging includes images — used to explain multimodal messaging.
  - The model as a "brain" and the API key / model access as part of that brain ecosystem.
  - "Biggest bottleneck" for learners is access to models / keys (the “brain” part).

- Any code, commands, or config shown
  - No explicit code snippets; references only:
    - Model filter action: "search on models and go free" (UI/search instruction).
    - Log entry identifier example: "R10528" (indicates an Open Router call).
    - Reference to obtaining an API/key from Open Router: "just get your key".
    - Mention of "pyic base model" (probably referring to a base model class for structured output — transcript text: "pyic base model").

- Warnings, gotchas, or important caveats
  - Free models require no payment; using paid models without proper billing/keys will fail and prompt for money.
  - Not all models support multimodal/image input — check model capabilities (GPT-3.5 Turbo and some small models do not support images).
  - Syntax for integrating with Open Router or specifying structured output can be non-obvious and may take time to figure out.
  - If you rely on privacy, use local models (e.g., local LLaMA) rather than remote services that may see your data.
  - The speaker implies some ambiguity/typos in terminology ("multimodel" vs "multimodal") — be careful with terms.

- Comparisons or lists (potential table candidates)
  - Three model approaches (compare by hosting, privacy, cost):
    - ChatGPT: remote, paid (when using GPT-4 etc.), powerful.
    - Local LLaMA: local hosting, private, may be less capable or require resources.
    - Open Router: remote aggregator, multiple models via providers, can use free models without credit card.
  - Model image-support comparison:
    - Likely no image support: GPT-3.5 Turbo, Tiny Llama, many older/smaller models.
    - Image-capable: GPT-4, DeepS v3 (via Open Router).
  - Message types in autogen (listable columns): text message, image message, tool message, chat message, multimodal message, task result (special output).
  - Use-cases for multimodal input:
    - Food delivery / e-commerce (users upload images of food/items)
    - Any agent needing to interpret visual input

- Direct quotes worth preserving
  - "We have the entry for R10528 which means that yes the call is going to open router"
  - "it is totally free and it is not asking you for any money"
  - "Open router works by providing you access via some providers"
  - "it will not ask you anything until and unless you are using free models. The second you are using any paid model surely it will fail and ask you for some money."
  - "we have learned that how we can use a chad GPT as the paid option for our brain"
  - "local lama for those people who want to get everything on their system and they're always kind of like okay I don't want to give my data. So local is also supported."
  - "finally we have another very awesome service in terms of open router. Why you just get your key and you have multiple models which you can choose."
  - "you cannot make any excuse that okay you don't have an API key or your laptop is pretty slow."
  - "Chapter 9: Multimodal Input"
  - "messages as the way agents communicate like the how we text our friend."
  - "10 years back there was just simple SMS. Now we have the way through which we can send the images as well."
  - "text message, image message or tool message."
  - "there is one very important which is your task result."
